```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T14:15:57.394Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_vPsrr3PlWpsbcXkeHv6ujP25",
    "output": "Chunk ID: da4310\nWall time: 0.0505 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 5144\nOutput:\n# SPEC_4.md — FR4: Seat Selection → Pricing Tier Validation → Cart Assembly\n\n**Owner:** booking-inventory (primary), payments-ticketing (downstream consumer)\n**Module(s):** `booking-inventory` (primary), `shared/eventbrite` (`EbTicketService`), `payments-ticketing` (event consumer), `scheduling` (REST read-only), `discovery-catalog` (REST read-only for seat floor plan)\n**Flow:** Flow 4 — Seat Availability Read → Soft Lock → Pricing Validation → Cart Assembly → Hard Lock → Eventbrite Checkout Handoff\n**Last Updated:** March 4, 2026\n**Status:** Stages 1–7 Complete — All pre-FR4 gaps resolved ✅  \n**Interface Migration (2026-03-04):** scheduling → booking-inventory REST reads replaced with shared reader interfaces ✅\n\n**Depends on:**\n- `SPEC_2.md` (FR2) — `show_slot.status = ACTIVE`, `eb_event_id` populated, `show_slot_pricing_tier` rows with `eb_ticket_class_id` must all exist before FR4 is reachable\n- `SPEC_3.MD` (FR3) — JWT with `sub=userId`, `role` claims required for all protected endpoints\n- `SPEC_AUTH.MD` — `JwtAuthenticationFilter` + `SecurityConfig` in place\n\n**Pre-FR4 Changes Implemented (as of 2026-03-04):**\n- ✅ `orgId` JWT claim added to `JwtTokenProvider` + `AuthenticatedUser` principal record in `shared/`\n- ✅ `JwtAuthenticationFilter` now sets `AuthenticatedUser(userId, role, orgId)` as `SecurityContext` principal\n- ✅ `ShowSlotActivatedEvent` now carries `seatingMode` as 6th component\n- ✅ `CartAssembledEvent` record created in `shared/` with full payload `(cartId, slotId, userId, orgId, ebEventId, couponCode)`\n- ✅ `GET /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{slotId}/pricing-tiers` endpoint implemented in `scheduling`\n- ✅ `GET /api/v1/catalog/venues/{venueId}/seat-layout` endpoint + `VenueSeat` entity + `VenueSeatRepository` implemented in `discovery-catalog`\n- ✅ `show_slot_pricing_tier` group discount columns added (V17 migration)\n- ✅ `venue_seat` table created (V18 migration)\n- ✅ `SecurityConfig` public routes updated: `/api/v1/booking/slots/*/seats` and `/api/v1/booking/slots/*/availability` are `permitAll()`\n\n**Interface Migration Implemented (as of 2026-03-04):**\n- ✅ `SlotSummaryReader` + `SlotPricingReader` interfaces added to `shared/common/service/`\n- ✅ `SlotSummaryDto` + `PricingTierDto` records added to `shared/common/dto/`\n- ✅ `SlotSummaryReaderImpl` + `SlotPricingReaderImpl` `@Service` beans added to `scheduling/service/`\n- ✅ All `booking-inventory` callers migrated from `SchedulingSlotClient` HTTP calls to shared reader interfaces\n- ✅ `GaInventoryService.initCounters()` signature updated to accept `List<PricingTierDto>`\n- ✅ `CartPricingService` extended with `getSlotPricingCached(Long slotId)` — cache-aside via `StringRedisTemplate` + `ObjectMapper`\n- ✅ `SchedulingSlotClient`, `SchedulingSlotResponse`, `SchedulingPricingTierResponse` deleted from `booking-inventory`\n- ✅ `BookingInventoryArchTest` updated with two explicit named ArchUnit rules\n- ✅ `SlotSummaryReaderImplTest`, `SlotPricingReaderImplTest`, `CartPricingServiceTest`, `SlotTicketSyncServiceTest` added/updated\n\n---\n\n## Stage 1 — Requirements\n\n### Area A — Pre-conditions & Slot Guard\n\n- `[MUST]` Before exposing seat availability, verify `show_slot.status = ACTIVE` via REST GET to `scheduling`.\n- `[MUST]` If `eb_event_id` is null on the slot, return `503 SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE` with `EB_EVENT_NOT_SYNCED` — never `404`. *(PRODUCT.md Hard Rule #10)*\n- `[MUST]` If any pricing tier in the cart has a null `eb_ticket_class_id`, block cart confirm with `TIER_NOT_SYNCED`.\n- `[MUST]` If org token is not in `CONNECTED` state (`EbTokenStore.getValidToken(orgId)` throws `EbAuthException`), block checkout initiation.\n- `[MUST]` Reject requests from users whose `status != ACTIVE`.\n- `[MUST]` Reject a second `PENDING` cart for the same `(userId, slotId)` — enforced by partial unique index on `carts`.\n\n### Area B — Seat Availability Read\n\n- `[MUST]` Expose `GET /api/v1/booking/shows/{slotId}/available-seats`.\n- `[MUST]` For `RESERVED` seating: return seats where `lock_state = AVAILABLE` OR (`lock_state = SOFT_LOCKED` AND `locked_until < NOW()`).\n- `[MUST]` For `GA` seating: return per-tier quota and available quantity (`quota - confirmed_count`), resolved via REST to `scheduling`.\n- `[MUST]` No Eventbrite call on this path — internal DB only. Eventbrite `quantity_sold` lags and does not include in-flight checkouts.\n\n### Area C — Seat Selection / Soft Lock\n\n- `[MUST]` Expose `POST /api/v1/booking/cart/add-seat` (JWT `ROLE_USER`).\n- `[MUST]` Acquire Redis lock atomically via Lua script (`SET NX PX`) before any DB write.\n- `[MUST]` If Redis NX returns `CONFLICT`, throw `SeatAlreadyLockedException` — return `409` with up to 5 alternative seats from same section/tier.\n- `[MUST]` On successful Redis lock, DB-update seat to `SOFT_LOCKED` with `locked_until = NOW() + 5 min`.\n- `[MUST]` If DB write fails after Redis lock acquired, rollback Redis key (`DEL` via ownership-checked Lua) before rethrowing.\n- `[MUST]` Upsert `Cart` (one per `(userId, slotId)`, `status = PENDING`); create `CartItem`.\n- `[MUST]` For GA: decrement Redis atomic counter (`ga:available:{slotId}:{tierId}`) via Lua `DECRBY`; reject with `INSUFFICIENT_GA_INVENTORY` if available < requested.\n- `[MUST]` Handle double-submit idempotency: Redis `ALREADY_YOURS` result → treat as success; `CartItem` upsert on `(cart_id, seat_id)` is a no-op if row exists.\n- `[SHOULD]` Expose `DELETE /api/v1/booking/cart/items/{itemId}` that fires `RELEASE` event and recomputes cart total.\n\n### Area D — Pricing Tier Validation & Cart Total\n\n- `[MUST]` Recompute cart total inline on every `add-seat` and `remove-seat` call — never deferred.\n- `[MUST]` Pricing source of truth is `show_slot_pricing_tier` owned by `scheduling` — accessed via `SlotPricingReader` shared interface (implemented in `scheduling`, injected into `booking-inventory`), never via direct REST call from `booking-inventory` and never imported as a JPA entity.\n- `[MUST]` Apply group discount per tier: if `cartItemsForTier >= groupDiscountThreshold`, apply `groupDiscountPercent` to ALL items of that tier retroactively.\n- `[MUST]` Removing a seat that drops a tier count below threshold removes the discount from all remaining items of that tier retroactively.\n- `[MUST]` Group discount is always recomputed from scratch — never incrementally accumulated.\n- `[SHOULD]` Return `CartResponse` including line items, per-tier subtotals, discount breakdown, and `cartTotal`.\n\n### Area E — Cart Confirm / Hard Lock\n\n- `[MUST]` Expose `POST /api/v1/booking/cart/confirm` (JWT `ROLE_USER`).\n- `[MUST]` Verify `cart.status = PENDING` and `cart.expires_at > NOW()`.\n- `[MUST]` Verify Redis lock is still owned by this user for every seat via ownership-checked Lua GET.\n- `[MUST]` Call `EbTicketService.getTicketClass(ebEventId, ebTicketClassId)` — this is the **only Eventbrite call on the hot path** — verify `on_sale_status = AVAILABLE` per unique tier in cart.\n- `[MUST]` If any tier is `SOLD_OUT` on Eventbrite: release all soft locks for that tier, return `409 TIER_SOLD_OUT` with alternative tiers.\n- `[MUST]` Transition all seats `SOFT_LOCKED → HARD_LOCKED`, extend `locked_until = NOW() + 30 min`.\n- `[MUST]` Refresh Redis TTLs to 1800 seconds and extend `cart.expires_at` to match.\n- `[MUST]` Publish `CartAssembledEvent(cartId, slotId, userId, orgId, ebEventId, couponCode)` — `payments-ticketing` listens.\n\n### Area F — Distributed Seat Lock State Machine\n\n- `[MUST]` Implement `SeatLockStateMachine` in `booking-inventory/statemachine/` using Spring State Machine factory.\n- `[MUST]` States: `AVAILABLE → SOFT_LOCKED → HARD_LOCKED → PAYMENT_PENDING → CONFIRMED`; `RELEASE` from any non-CONFIRMED state returns to `AVAILABLE`.\n- `[MUST]` Machine is rebuilt from `seats.lock_state` DB column on each call — no SSM Redis persistence store.\n- `[MUST]` All Redis operations (acquire, extend, release, GA claim/restore) are Lua scripts — no Java read-then-write races.\n- `[MUST]` Write to `seat_lock_audit_log` on every state transition — append-only, never update or delete rows.\n- `[MUST]` `AvailabilityGuard` (SELECT event) and `CartTtlGuard` (CONFIRM event) must be distinct Spring beans.\n\n### Area G — Background Cleanup Jobs\n\n- `[MUST]` `SoftLockCleanupJob` (every 60s): bulk UPDATE seats to `AVAILABLE` where `lock_state = SOFT_LOCKED AND locked_until < NOW()`; delete expired `ga_inventory_claims` and restore Redis counter via atomic `INCRBY`.\n- `[MUST]` `CartExpiryCleanupJob` (every 5 min): find `PENDING` carts where `expires_at < NOW()` → set `EXPIRED`; fire `RELEASE` on all `SOFT_LOCKED` seats; do NOT touch `HARD_LOCKED` or `PAYMENT_PENDING` seats.\n- `[MUST]` `PaymentTimeoutWatchdog` (every 60s): find `HARD_LOCKED`/`PAYMENT_PENDING` seats where `locked_until < NOW()`; call `EbOrderService` to verify no placed order; if none → fire `RELEASE(reason=PAYMENT_TIMEOUT)`; publish `PaymentTimeoutEvent`.\n- `[SHOULD]` `EbInventoryValidationJob` (every 2 min): call `EbTicketService.listTicketClasses(ebEventId)` per ACTIVE slot; if any tier `on_sale_status = SOLD_OUT` → write `SET tier:blocked:{slotId}:{tierId} 1 EX 300` to Redis; emit `InventoryDriftDetectedEvent`.\n\n### Area H — Spring Events (Inter-Module)\n\n- `[MUST]` Listen to `ShowSlotActivatedEvent(slotId, ebEventId, orgId, venueId, seatingMode)` from `scheduling` to provision seats (RESERVED) or initialize GA Redis counters.\n- `[MUST]` Publish `CartAssembledEvent(cartId, slotId, userId, orgId, ebEventId, couponCode)` after hard lock transition. *(Hard Rule #4 — primitives/IDs only)*\n- `[MUST]` Listen to `BookingConfirmedEvent(cartId, List<seatId>, ebOrderId, userId)` from `payments-ticketing` → batch-transition seats `PAYMENT_PENDING → CONFIRMED`.\n- `[MUST]` Listen to `PaymentFailedEvent(cartId, List<seatId>, userId)` from `payments-ticketing` → fire `RELEASE` on all seats.\n- `[MUST]` Publish `PaymentTimeoutEvent(cartId, List<seatId>, userId)` when watchdog releases locks — `payments-ticketing` marks booking attempt abandoned.\n\n### Area I — Conflict Resolution\n\n- `[MUST]` On `SEAT_UNAVAILABLE` (409): return up to 5 nearest alternatives in same section/tier + up to 3 from adjacent section.\n- `[MUST]` On `SOFT_LOCK_EXPIRED` (410): return list of expired `seatIds` and prompt re-selection from updated availability.\n- `[SHOULD]` On `TIER_SOLD_OUT` (409): return available alternative tiers or nearby slots.\n\n### Area J — Out of Scope\n\n- `[WONT]` Payment processing — owned by `payments-ticketing` (FR5).\n- `[WONT]` Coupon/promotion application — owned by `promotions` (FR7).\n- `[WONT]` E-ticket generation — owned by `payments-ticketing`.\n- `[WONT]` Eventbrite seat map creation — seats provisioned from internal venue floor plan only.\n- `[WONT]` Admin seat override or manual lock controls.\n\n---\n\n## Stage 2 — Domain Model\n\n### Domain Summary\n\nA `Cart` belongs to one user (scalar `user_id`) and one `ShowSlot` (scalar `show_slot_id`). Only one `PENDING` cart may exist per `(user, slot)` pair at a time, enforced by a partial unique index. A `Cart` has many `CartItems`. In RESERVED mode, each `CartItem` references one `Seat` that progresses through the lock state machine. In GA mode, each `CartItem` references one `GaInventoryClaim` representing a quantity reservation against a tier pool. A `GroupDiscountRule` governs whether a tier's items qualify for bulk pricing. Every state transition on a `Seat` or `GaInventoryClaim` writes one immutable row to `SeatLockAuditLog`.\n\n### Entities Owned by `booking-inventory`\n\n**Seat** *(RESERVED seating only)*\n- Identity: `id` (BIGSERIAL); unique by `(show_slot_id, seat_number)`\n- Lifecycle: `AVAILABLE → SOFT_LOCKED → HARD_LOCKED → PAYMENT_PENDING → CONFIRMED`; any non-CONFIRMED state can RELEASE back to `AVAILABLE`\n- Relationships: belongs to one `show_slot` (scalar ID — no JPA FK to `scheduling`); belongs to one pricing tier (scalar `pricing_tier_id`); optionally referenced by one `CartItem`\n- Ownership: `booking-inventory`; provisioned from internal venue floor plan (`discovery-catalog` `VenueSeat` entity) on `ShowSlotActivatedEvent`\n- Domain methods: `softLock(userId, ttl)`, `hardLock(ttl)`, `markPaymentPending()`, `confirm(ebOrderId)`, `release(reason)`\n\n**GaInventoryClaim** *(GA seating only)*\n- Identity: `id`; unique by `(show_slot_id, pricing_tier_id, user_id, cart_id)`\n- Lifecycle: `SOFT_LOCKED → HARD_LOCKED → CONFIRMED` or released\n- Relationships: belongs to one `show_slot` and one pricing tier (both scalar IDs); linked to one `Cart`\n- Ownership: `booking-inventory`\n\n**Cart**\n- Identity: `id`; business identity `(user_id, show_slot_id)` where `status = PENDING` (partial unique index)\n- Lifecycle: `PENDING → CONFIRMED | ABANDONED | EXPIRED`\n- Relationships: one `Cart` has many `CartItems`; references one `show_slot` (scalar ID); references one user (scalar `user_id`)\n- Ownership: `booking-inventory`\n- Domain methods: `confirm()`, `abandon()`, `expire()`, `isExpired()`, `extendTtl(minutes)`\n\n**CartItem**\n- Identity: `id`\n- Lifecycle: created on seat selection; removed on seat removal; finalized when cart confirms\n- Relationships: belongs to one `Cart`; references one `Seat` (RESERVED — NOT NULL) or one `GaInventoryClaim` (GA — NOT NULL); stores scalar `pricing_tier_id` + denormalized `eb_ticket_class_id`\n- Ownership: `booking-inventory`\n- Constraint: exactly one of `seat_id` or `ga_claim_id` must be set (CHECK constraint)\n\n**GroupDiscountRule**\n- Identity: `id`; unique by `(show_slot_id, pricing_tier_id)`\n- Lifecycle: created when slot is activated from `ShowSlotActivatedEvent` pricing tier data; updated if admin edits pricing\n- Relationships: one rule per pricing tier per slot; consumed by `CartPricingService`\n- Ownership: `booking-inventory`; mirrors `scheduling`'s `show_slot_pricing_tier` discount fields — stored here to avoid REST calls on every cart compute\n\n**SeatLockAuditLog**\n- Identity: `id` (BIGSERIAL, append-only)\n- Lifecycle: immutable — one row per state transition, never updated or deleted\n- Relationships: references `seat_id` (nullable — null for GA), `ga_claim_id` (nullable), `show_slot_id`, `user_id`\n- Ownership: `booking-inventory`\n\n### Non-Persisted Operational Concepts\n\n**SeatLockStateMachine** *(in-memory per request)*\n- Not persisted in SSM store; rebuilt from `seats.lock_state` DB column on each invocation\n- Guards: `AvailabilityGuard` (SELECT), `CartTtlGuard` (CONFIRM)\n- Actions: `SoftLockAction`, `HardLockAction`, `PaymentPendingAction`, `ConfirmAction`, `ReleaseAction`\n\n**Redis Seat Lock** *(key: `seat:lock:{seatId}` / `ga:available:{slotId}:{tierId}`)*\n- Not persisted in DB; TTL-based; all read-modify-write operations via Lua scripts only\n- RESERVED: NX key per seat, value = `userId` string\n- GA: atomic integer counter per `(slotId, tierId)`\n\n**Tier Blocked Flag** *(key: `tier:blocked:{slotId}:{tierId}`)*\n- Written by `EbInventoryValidationJob` when Eventbrite reports `SOLD_OUT`\n- TTL = 300s (5 min); gates new soft locks for the affected tier\n\n### Read-Only External References (not owned by this module)\n\n- `ShowSlotPricingTier` — owned by `scheduling`; accessed via `SlotPricingReader` shared interface (`SlotPricingReaderImpl` bean in `scheduling`); cached in `booking-inventory` via `CartPricingService.getSlotPricingCached()` — `StringRedisTemplate` JSON cache, key `pricing:slot:{slotId}`, TTL 10 min. REST endpoint `GET /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{slotId}/pricing-tiers` still exists for admin/external consumers only.\n- `ShowSlot` metadata — owned by `scheduling`; accessed via `SlotSummaryReader` shared interface (`SlotSummaryReaderImpl` bean in `scheduling`); no caching (fast single-row read, called only on validation path).\n- `User` — owned by `identity`; scalar `user_id` only; JWT claims used for authentication\n- `VenueSeat` — owned by `discovery-catalog`; read via REST `GET /api/v1/catalog/venues/{venueId}/seat-layout` for seat provisioning (provisioning path only — not hot path)\n\n---\n\n## Stage 3 — Architecture & File Structure\n\n### Module Ownership\n- **Primary:** `booking-inventory`\n- **Event consumers added:** `payments-ticketing` (new listeners for `CartAssembledEvent`)\n- **Shared reader interfaces (in-process, no HTTP):** `SlotSummaryReader` → `scheduling/SlotSummaryReaderImpl`; `SlotPricingReader` → `scheduling/SlotPricingReaderImpl`\n- **REST reads (provisioning path only):** `discovery-catalog` `GET /api/v1/catalog/venues/{venueId}/seat-layout` via `CatalogSeatLayoutClient`\n- **ACL facade used:** `shared/eventbrite/service/EbTicketService` (existing), `EbOrderService` (existing, watchdog only)\n\n### Feature File Structure\n\n```\nbooking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/\n\n├── api/\n│   ├── controller/\n│   │   ├── SeatAvailabilityController.java      ← GET  /api/v1/booking/shows/{slotId}/available-seats\n│   │   └── CartController.java                  ← POST add-seat, DELETE item, POST confirm, POST abandon, GET cart\n│   └── dto/\n│       ├── request/\n│       │   ├── AddSeatRequest.java              ← slotId, seatId (nullable), tierId, quantity\n│       │   ├── RemoveSeatRequest.java           ← cartId, itemId\n│       │   └── ConfirmCartRequest.java          ← cartId\n│       └── response/\n│           ├── AvailableSeatResponse.java       ← seatId, seatNumber, rowLabel, section, tierId, basePrice, lockState\n│           ├── GaTierAvailabilityResponse.java  ← tierId, tierName, quota, available, basePrice\n│           ├── CartResponse.java                ← cartId, slotId, status, expiresAt, items, subtotal, discountAmount, total, currency\n│           ├── CartItemResponse.java            ← itemId, seatId (nullable), tierId, tierName, quantity, unitPrice, discountApplied\n│           └── SeatAlternativesResponse.java    ← unavailableSeatId, sameSection: List, adjacentSection: List\n\n├── domain/\n│   ├── Seat.java\n│   ├── GaInventoryClaim.java\n│   ├── Cart.java\n│   ├── CartItem.java\n│   ├── GroupDiscountRule.java\n│   ├── SeatLockAuditLog.java\n│   └── enums/\n│       ├── SeatLockState.java        ← AVAILABLE, SOFT_LOCKED, HARD_LOCKED, PAYMENT_PENDING, CONFIRMED, RELEASED\n│       ├── SeatLockEvent.java        ← SELECT, CONFIRM, CHECKOUT_INITIATE, CONFIRM_PAYMENT, RELEASE\n│       ├── CartStatus.java           ← PENDING, CONFIRMED, ABANDONED, EXPIRED\n│       ├── SeatingMode.java          ← RESERVED, GA\n│       └── LockReleaseReason.java    ← TTL_EXPIRED, USER_REMOVED, CART_ABANDONED, PAYMENT_FAILED, ORDER_ABANDONED, PAYMENT_TIMEOUT, TIER_SOLD_OUT\n\n├── repository/\n│   ├── SeatRepository.java                      ← findBySlotIdAndLockState, findExpiredSoftLocks, findByIdWithLock (SELECT FOR UPDATE)\n│   ├── GaInventoryClaimRepository.java\n│   ├── CartRepository.java                      ← findPendingByUserAndSlot\n│   ├── CartItemRepository.java\n│   ├── GroupDiscountRuleRepository.java\n│   └── SeatLockAuditLogRepository.java          ← save only — no updates\n\n├── service/\n│   ├── SeatAvailabilityService.java             ← reads DB, assembles seat map (RESERVED) or GA tier view; tiers via CartPricingService.getSlotPricingCached()\n│   ├── CartService.java                         ← orchestrates add-seat, remove-seat, confirm, abandon; tiers via CartPricingService.getSlotPricingCached()\n│   ├── CartPricingService.java                  ← group discount full recompute; getSlotPricingCached(Long slotId) — cache-aside (key: pricing:slot:{slotId}, TTL 10 min, StringRedisTemplate+ObjectMapper)\n│   ├── SlotValidationService.java               ← uses SlotSummaryReader; requireActiveAndSynced() returns SlotSummaryDto\n│   ├── SeatProvisioningService.java             ← listens ShowSlotActivatedEvent; uses SlotPricingReader directly (provisioning path, no cache needed); bulk-inserts Seat rows (RESERVED); inits GA counters\n│   ├── GaInventoryService.java                  ← initCounters(Long slotId, List<PricingTierDto> tiers); manages ga_inventory_claims + Redis quantity counters\n│   ├── SlotTicketSyncService.java               ← uses SlotSummaryReader + SlotPricingReader (two separate reads)\n"
  }
}
```

`SEAT_UNAVAILABLE` (409): return up to 5 nearest alternatives in same section/tier + up to 3 from adjacent section.\n- `[MUST]` On `SOFT_LOCK_EXPIRED` (410): return list of expired `seatIds` and prompt re-selection from updated availability.\n- `[SHOULD]` On `TIER_SOLD_OUT` (409): return available alternative tiers or nearby slots.\n\n### Area J — Out of Scope\n\n- `[WONT]` Payment processing — owned by `payments-ticketing` (FR5).\n- `[WONT]` Coupon/promotion application — owned by `promotions` (FR7).\n- `[WONT]` E-ticket generation — owned by `payments-ticketing`.\n- `[WONT]` Eventbrite seat map creation — seats provisioned from internal venue floor plan only.\n- `[WONT]` Admin seat override or manual lock controls.\n\n---\n\n## Stage 2 — Domain Model\n\n### Domain Summary\n\nA `Cart` belongs to one user (scalar `user_id`) and one `ShowSlot` (scalar `show_slot_id`). Only one `PENDING` cart may exist per `(user, slot)` pair at a time, enforced by a partial unique index. A `Cart` has many `CartItems`. In RESERVED mode, each `CartItem` references one `Seat` that progresses through the lock state machine. In GA mode, each `CartItem` references one `GaInventoryClaim` representing a quantity reservation against a tier pool. A `GroupDiscountRule` governs whether a tier's items qualify for bulk pricing. Every state transition on a `Seat` or `GaInventoryClaim` writes one immutable row to `SeatLockAuditLog`.\n\n### Entities Owned by `booking-inventory`\n\n**Seat** *(RESERVED seating only)*\n- Identity: `id` (BIGSERIAL); unique by `(show_slot_id, seat_number)`\n- Lifecycle: `AVAILABLE → SOFT_LOCKED → HARD_LOCKED → PAYMENT_PENDING → CONFIRMED`; any non-CONFIRMED state can RELEASE back to `AVAILABLE`\n- Relationships: belongs to one `show_slot` (scalar ID — no JPA FK to `scheduling`); belongs to one pricing tier (scalar `pricing_tier_id`); optionally referenced by one `CartItem`\n- Ownership: `booking-inventory`; provisioned from internal venue floor plan (`discovery-catalog` `VenueSeat` entity) on `ShowSlotActivatedEvent`\n- Domain methods: `softLock(userId, ttl)`, `hardLock(ttl)`, `markPaymentPending()`, `confirm(ebOrderId)`, `release(reason)`\n\n**GaInventoryClaim** *(GA seating only)*\n- Identity: `id`; unique by `(show_slot_id, pricing_tier_id, user_id, cart_id)`\n- Lifecycle: `SOFT_LOCKED → HARD_LOCKED → CONFIRMED` or released\n- Relationships: belongs to one `show_slot` and one pricing tier (both scalar IDs); linked to one `Cart`\n- Ownership: `booking-inventory`\n\n**Cart**\n- Identity: `id`; business identity `(user_id, show_slot_id)` where `status = PENDING` (partial unique index)\n- Lifecycle: `PENDING → CONFIRMED | ABANDONED | EXPIRED`\n- Relationships: one `Cart` has many `CartItems`; references one `show_slot` (scalar ID); references one user (scalar `user_id`)\n- Ownership: `booking-inventory`\n- Domain methods: `confirm()`, `abandon()`, `expire()`, `isExpired()`, `extendTtl(minutes)`\n\n**CartItem**\n- Identity: `id`\n- Lifecycle: created on seat selection; removed on seat removal; finalized when cart confirms\n- Relationships: belongs to one `Cart`; references one `Seat` (RESERVED — NOT NULL) or one `GaInventoryClaim` (GA — NOT NULL); stores scalar `pricing_tier_id` + denormalized `eb_ticket_class_id`\n- Ownership: `booking-inventory`\n- Constraint: exactly one of `seat_id` or `ga_claim_id` must be set (CHECK constraint)\n\n**GroupDiscountRule**\n- Identity: `id`; unique by `(show_slot_id, pricing_tier_id)`\n- Lifecycle: created when slot is activated from `ShowSlotActivatedEvent` pricing tier data; updated if admin edits pricing\n- Relationships: one rule per pricing tier per slot; consumed by `CartPricingService`\n- Ownership: `booking-inventory`; mirrors `scheduling`'s `show_slot_pricing_tier` discount fields — stored here to avoid REST calls on every cart compute\n\n**SeatLockAuditLog**\n- Identity: `id` (BIGSERIAL, append-only)\n- Lifecycle: immutable — one row per state transition, never updated or deleted\n- Relationships: references `seat_id` (nullable — null for GA), `ga_claim_id` (nullable), `show_slot_id`, `user_id`\n- Ownership: `booking-inventory`\n\n### Non-Persisted Operational Concepts\n\n**SeatLockStateMachine** *(in-memory per request)*\n- Not persisted in SSM store; rebuilt from `seats.lock_state` DB column on each invocation\n- Guards: `AvailabilityGuard` (SELECT), `CartTtlGuard` (CONFIRM)\n- Actions: `SoftLockAction`, `HardLockAction`, `PaymentPendingAction`, `ConfirmAction`, `ReleaseAction`\n\n**Redis Seat Lock** *(key: `seat:lock:{seatId}` / `ga:available:{slotId}:{tierId}`)*\n- Not persisted in DB; TTL-based; all read-modify-write operations via Lua scripts only\n- RESERVED: NX key per seat, value = `userId` string\n- GA: atomic integer counter per `(slotId, tierId)`\n\n**Tier Blocked Flag** *(key: `tier:blocked:{slotId}:{tierId}`)*\n- Written by `EbInventoryValidationJob` when Eventbrite reports `SOLD_OUT`\n- TTL = 300s (5 min); gates new soft locks for the affected tier\n\n### Read-Only External References (not owned by this module)\n\n- `ShowSlotPricingTier` — owned by `scheduling`; accessed via `SlotPricingReader` shared interface (`SlotPricingReaderImpl` bean in `scheduling`); cached in `booking-inventory` via `CartPricingService.getSlotPricingCached()` — `StringRedisTemplate` JSON cache, key `pricing:slot:{slotId}`, TTL 10 min. REST endpoint `GET /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{slotId}/pricing-tiers` still exists for admin/external consumers only.\n- `ShowSlot` metadata — owned by `scheduling`; accessed via `SlotSummaryReader` shared interface (`SlotSummaryReaderImpl` bean in `scheduling`); no caching (fast single-row read, called only on validation path).\n- `User` — owned by `identity`; scalar `user_id` only; JWT claims used for authentication\n- `VenueSeat` — owned by `discovery-catalog`; read via REST `GET /api/v1/catalog/venues/{venueId}/seat-layout` for seat provisioning (provisioning path only — not hot path)\n\n---\n\n## Stage 3 — Architecture & File Structure\n\n### Module Ownership\n- **Primary:** `booking-inventory`\n- **Event consumers added:** `payments-ticketing` (new listeners for `CartAssembledEvent`)\n- **Shared reader interfaces (in-process, no HTTP):** `SlotSummaryReader` → `scheduling/SlotSummaryReaderImpl`; `SlotPricingReader` → `scheduling/SlotPricingReaderImpl`\n- **REST reads (provisioning path only):** `discovery-catalog` `GET /api/v1/catalog/venues/{venueId}/seat-layout` via `CatalogSeatLayoutClient`\n- **ACL facade used:** `shared/eventbrite/service/EbTicketService` (existing), `EbOrderService` (existing, watchdog only)\n\n### Feature File Structure\n\n```\nbooking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/\n\n├── api/\n│   ├── controller/\n│   │   ├── SeatAvailabilityController.java      ← GET  /api/v1/booking/shows/{slotId}/available-seats\n│   │   └── CartController.java                  ← POST add-seat, DELETE item, POST confirm, POST abandon, GET cart\n│   └── dto/\n│       ├── request/\n│       │   ├── AddSeatRequest.java              ← slotId, seatId (nullable), tierId, quantity\n│       │   ├── RemoveSeatRequest.java           ← cartId, itemId\n│       │   └── ConfirmCartRequest.java          ← cartId\n│       └── response/\n│           ├── AvailableSeatResponse.java       ← seatId, seatNumber, rowLabel, section, tierId, basePrice, lockState\n│           ├── GaTierAvailabilityResponse.java  ← tierId, tierName, quota, available, basePrice\n│           ├── CartResponse.java                ← cartId, slotId, status, expiresAt, items, subtotal, discountAmount, total, currency\n│           ├── CartItemResponse.java            ← itemId, seatId (nullable), tierId, tierName, quantity, unitPrice, discountApplied\n│           └── SeatAlternativesResponse.java    ← unavailableSeatId, sameSection: List, adjacentSection: List\n\n├── domain/\n│   ├── Seat.java\n│   ├── GaInventoryClaim.java\n│   ├── Cart.java\n│   ├── CartItem.java\n│   ├── GroupDiscountRule.java\n│   ├── SeatLockAuditLog.java\n│   └── enums/\n│       ├── SeatLockState.java        ← AVAILABLE, SOFT_LOCKED, HARD_LOCKED, PAYMENT_PENDING, CONFIRMED, RELEASED\n│       ├── SeatLockEvent.java        ← SELECT, CONFIRM, CHECKOUT_INITIATE, CONFIRM_PAYMENT, RELEASE\n│       ├── CartStatus.java           ← PENDING, CONFIRMED, ABANDONED, EXPIRED\n│       ├── SeatingMode.java          ← RESERVED, GA\n│       └── LockReleaseReason.java    ← TTL_EXPIRED, USER_REMOVED, CART_ABANDONED, PAYMENT_FAILED, ORDER_ABANDONED, PAYMENT_TIMEOUT, TIER_SOLD_OUT\n\n├── repository/\n│   ├── SeatRepository.java                      ← findBySlotIdAndLockState, findExpiredSoftLocks, findByIdWithLock (SELECT FOR UPDATE)\n│   ├── GaInventoryClaimRepository.java\n│   ├── CartRepository.java                      ← findPendingByUserAndSlot\n│   ├── CartItemRepository.java\n│   ├── GroupDiscountRuleRepository.java\n│   └── SeatLockAuditLogRepository.java          ← save only — no updates\n\n├── service/\n│   ├── SeatAvailabilityService.java             ← reads DB, assembles seat map (RESERVED) or GA tier view; tiers via CartPricingService.getSlotPricingCached()\n│   ├── CartService.java                         ← orchestrates add-seat, remove-seat, confirm, abandon; tiers via CartPricingService.getSlotPricingCached()\n│   ├── CartPricingService.java                  ← group discount full recompute; getSlotPricingCached(Long slotId) — cache-aside (key: pricing:slot:{slotId}, TTL 10 min, StringRedisTemplate+ObjectMapper)\n│   ├── SlotValidationService.java               ← uses SlotSummaryReader; requireActiveAndSynced() returns SlotSummaryDto\n│   ├── SeatProvisioningService.java             ← listens ShowSlotActivatedEvent; uses SlotPricingReader directly (provisioning path, no cache needed); bulk-inserts Seat rows (RESERVED); inits GA counters\n│   ├── GaInventoryService.java                  ← initCounters(Long slotId, List<PricingTierDto> tiers); manages ga_inventory_claims + Redis quantity counters\n│   ├── SlotTicketSyncService.java               ← uses SlotSummaryReader + SlotPricingReader (two separate reads)\n"
  }
}
```

    "output": "Chunk ID: 96e19e\nWall time: 0.0504 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 589\nOutput:\n24:| booking-inventory | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Seat Lock State Machine, Pricing | Payments, Reviews, Users |\n33:## 2. Module Dependency Order\n44:booking-inventory     ← depends on shared + discovery-catalog (reads event/venue via REST)\n46:payments-ticketing    ← depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (reads via REST)\n48:promotions            ← depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (reads via REST)\n67:- booking-inventory → publishes `CartAssembledEvent`\n71:- booking-inventory → listens `PaymentFailedListener` → triggers seat lock rollback\n124:### Mock Eventbrite Service — Development & Testing\n357:**Publishes:** `ShowSlotCreatedEvent` → booking-inventory | `ShowSlotCancelledEvent` → booking-inventory + payments-ticketing\n388:**Module:** booking-inventory\n420:**Seat Lock State Machine — `booking-inventory/statemachine/`**\n553:**Publishes:** `BookingConfirmedEvent` → engagement + identity | `PaymentFailedEvent` → booking-inventory  \n554:**Listens to:** `CartAssembledEvent` (from booking-inventory)\n587:  - `BookingCancelledEvent` published → booking-inventory releases seats\n615:**Publishes:** `BookingCancelledEvent` → booking-inventory  \n647:  - `CouponAppliedEvent` published → booking-inventory recomputes cart total\n665:**Publishes:** `CouponAppliedEvent` → booking-inventory\n705:**Module:** `admin/` (orchestrator) — delegates to scheduling, discovery-catalog, booking-inventory\n769:**Module:** booking-inventory — `booking-inventory/statemachine/`\n885:| booking-inventory | `CartAssembledEvent` | payments-ticketing | Cart ready → initiate Eventbrite checkout |\n886:| payments-ticketing | `PaymentFailedEvent` | booking-inventory | Payment failed → rollback Redis seat locks |\n889:| payments-ticketing | `BookingCancelledEvent` | booking-inventory | Cancellation → release Redis seat locks |\n890:| scheduling | `ShowSlotCreatedEvent` | booking-inventory | Slot created → provision seat map |\n891:| scheduling | `ShowSlotCancelledEvent` | booking-inventory | Slot cancelled → release all seats |\n893:| promotions | `CouponAppliedEvent` | booking-inventory | Coupon applied → recompute cart total |\n923:## 9. Hard Rules — Never Violate These\n941:**Java package naming:** module names with hyphens removed. Example: `booking-inventory` → `com.[org].bookinginventory`\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 40
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T14:16:05.353Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T14:16:05.353Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_8eBprt5idKyGtQJY1T4k3pqd",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 6875be\nWall time: 0.0595 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 3259\nOutput:\n# Modular Monolith — Canonical Folder Structure Template\n\n> **Purpose:** This document is the canonical reference for building any new feature or module\n> in this codebase. Every agent, developer, or tool that adds code MUST follow this structure\n> exactly. It enforces high cohesion, low coupling, and zero cyclic dependencies by design.\n\n---\n\n## Core Architectural Rules (Read Before Writing Any Code)\n\n### Rule 1 — Dependency Direction is One-Way and Downward Only\n```\nshared/          ← no dependencies on any module (foundational)\n    ↓\n[module-A]       ← may depend on shared only\n    ↓\n[module-B]       ← may depend on shared + module-A\n    ↓\n[module-C]       ← may depend on shared + module-A + module-B\n    ↓\nadmin/           ← thin orchestration layer, depends on all modules but owns no domain\n    ↓\napp/             ← entry point only, no business logic\n```\n**A module NEVER imports from a module below it or at the same level.**\n**If two modules need to talk, they do so through Spring Events — never direct service calls.**\n\n### Rule 2 — Three Ways Modules Communicate (Pick the Right One)\n| Communication Type | When to Use | Mechanism |\n|---|---|---|\n| Query (sync, read-only) | Module B needs data that Module A owns | Module B calls Module A's **public API endpoint** (HTTP), never imports Module A's service class directly |\n| Command (async, fire-and-forget) | Module A completed something and others should react | Module A publishes a **Spring ApplicationEvent**; interested modules register a listener |\n| Shared contract | Enums, base classes, standard DTOs used by 2+ modules | Lives in `shared/common/` only — never duplicated |\n\n### Rule 3 — The ACL is the Only Door to External Systems\nAny external API (Eventbrite, payment gateway, SMS provider, etc.) MUST be accessed\nexclusively through the `shared/` ACL package. No module ever calls an external API directly.\nIf a new external integration is needed, it goes in `shared/` first, then modules consume it\nvia the ACL facade services.\n\n### Rule 4 — A Module Owns Its Own Database Tables\nNo two modules share a JPA entity or database table. If Module B needs data from Module A's\ntable, it calls Module A's API — it does NOT import Module A's `@Entity` class or `@Repository`.\nViolating this rule creates hidden coupling at the database layer.\n\n### Rule 5 — The `admin/` Module Owns No Domain\nThe admin module only orchestrates and aggregates. It delegates all writes to the owning module.\nIt never defines its own `@Entity` or owns a database table. It is a read + orchestration layer.\n\nFor inter-module access, admin follows this rule:\n- **Reads** — admin MAY import a module's `@Service` bean directly (same JVM, no overhead)\n- **Writes** — admin MUST call the owning module's REST API (never bypass the module's own validation, events, and business rules)\n\n### Rule 6 — Mapping Logic Belongs in the Mapper Layer Only\nConverting domain objects to API response DTOs and request DTOs to domain objects must be done\nexclusively in the `mapper/` package. Controllers and services must never call `.builder()` on a\nresponse DTO directly or manually map fields.\n- **Controllers** — call service, return result. No mapping.\n- **Services** — orchestrate domain + infra. No mapping.\n- **Mappers** — the only place field translation happens. Use MapStruct.\n\n---\n\n## Project Layout\n\n```\n[project-root]/\n├── pom.xml                    ← Parent POM. All dependency versions managed here only.\n├── shared/                    ← Internal library. Never imports from any module.\n├── [module-name]/             ← One folder per bounded context.\n├── admin/                     ← Thin orchestration layer. Owns no domain, no tables.\n└── app/                       ← Spring Boot entry point. Zero business logic.\n```\n\n---\n\n## shared/ Structure\n\nCross-cutting concerns only. Every module depends on this.\n\n```\nshared/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/shared/\n    ├── [external-system-acl]/     ← One package per external system (e.g. razorpay/, eventbrite/)\n    │   ├── client/                ← Raw HTTP calls only\n    │   ├── config/                ← WebClient bean, rate limiter config\n    │   ├── dto/request/           ← External system's raw request shapes\n    │   ├── dto/response/          ← External system's raw response shapes\n    │   ├── mapper/                ← Translates external DTOs → internal domain objects\n    │   ├── service/               ← THE ONLY THING MODULES CALL. One service per capability.\n    │   ├── webhook/               ← Receives inbound webhooks, dispatches as Spring Events\n    │   └── resilience/            ← Circuit breaker + fallback config per external system\n    │\n    ├── security/\n    │   ├── JwtTokenProvider       ← Generate + validate JWTs\n    │   ├── JwtAuthenticationFilter← Spring Security filter, registered once\n    │   └── SecurityConstants      ← Token TTLs, header names, role constants\n    │\n    ├── common/\n    │   ├── domain/\n    │   │   ├── BaseEntity         ← id, createdAt, updatedAt — all entities extend this\n    │   │   └── Money              ← Value object for all monetary amounts. Never use primitives.\n    │   ├── dto/\n    │   │   ├── ApiResponse        ← Standard success response wrapper\n    │   │   ├── PageResponse       ← Paginated list wrapper\n    │   │   └── ErrorResponse      ← Standard error shape\n    │   ├── enums/                 ← Enums shared across 2+ modules only\n    │   └── exception/\n    │       ├── GlobalExceptionHandler  ← @ControllerAdvice. One handler for the entire app.\n    │       ├── ResourceNotFoundException   ← 404\n    │       ├── BusinessRuleException       ← 422\n    │       └── IntegrationException        ← 502\n    │\n    └── config/\n        ├── RedisConfig            ← Shared Redis bean\n        ├── ElasticsearchConfig    ← Shared ES client bean\n        ├── CacheConfig            ← Cache names and TTLs\n        ├── AsyncConfig            ← Thread pool for @Async\n        ├── OpenApiConfig          ← Springdoc global config\n        ├── WebConfig              ← Global CORS + request logging. Never use @CrossOrigin on controllers.\n        └── TracingConfig          ← MDC traceId on every log line. Essential for prod debugging.\n```\n\n---\n\n## [module-name]/ Structure\n\nOne folder per bounded context. Name reflects the domain, not the tech.\n\n```\n[module-name]/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/[module]/\n    ├── api/\n    │   ├── controller/            ← HTTP endpoints only. No business logic. Delegates to service.\n    │   └── dto/\n    │       ├── request/           ← [Action][Resource]Request\n    │       └── response/          ← [Resource]Response. Flat, serializable, no JPA proxies.\n    │\n    ├── domain/                    ← Pure business logic. No Spring, no JPA, no infrastructure.\n    │                                Entities have behavior (confirm(), cancel()) not just getters.\n    │\n    ├── service/                   ← Orchestrates domain + repositories + ACL + events. @Service beans.\n    │                                One service per capability group. Methods = use cases not CRUD.\n    │\n    ├── repository/                ← @Repository interfaces only. No other module imports these.\n    │\n    ├── mapper/                    ← Domain ↔ DTO translation only. Use MapStruct.\n    │\n    ├── event/\n    │   ├── published/             ← Events this module fires. Past tense. Primitives/IDs only.\n    │   └── listener/              ← Events this module reacts to. No logic — delegate to service.\n    │\n    ├── statemachine/              ← OPTIONAL. Only if 4+ states with conditional transition guards.\n    │   ├── [Resource]StateMachineConfig\n    │   ├── [Resource]State\n    │   ├── [Resource]Event\n    │   ├── [Resource]StateMachineService\n    │   ├── guard/\n    │   └── action/\n    │\n    └── exception/                 ← OPTIONAL. Module-specific exceptions only.\n```\n\n---\n\n## admin/ and app/\n\n```\nadmin/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/admin/\n    ├── api/controller/            ← Admin-only endpoints (@PreAuthorize ROLE_ADMIN)\n    └── service/                   ← Aggregates reads across modules. Delegates writes to module APIs.\n\napp/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/\n    ├── [ProjectName]Application   ← @SpringBootApplication. One class, nothing else.\n    └── resources/\n        ├── application.yml\n        ├── application-local.yml\n        ├── application-prod.yml\n        └── db/migration/          ← Flyway migrations. Global version sequence. Never modify existing files.\n```\n\n---\n\n## Anti-Patterns — Never Do These\n\n| Anti-Pattern | Why It's Wrong | Correct Approach |\n|---|---|---|\n| Module A imports `@Service` from Module B | Direct coupling — changes in B break A at compile time | Module A calls Module B's REST endpoint or listens to its Spring Event |\n| Module A imports `@Entity` or `@Repository` from Module B | Hidden DB coupling — schema changes in B break A silently | Module A calls Module B's API to get the data it needs |\n| Putting Eventbrite/Razorpay HTTP calls inside a module's service | Scattered integration — if the API changes, you hunt across modules | All external calls go through `shared/[system-acl]/service/` facades only |\n| Sharing a database table between two modules | Breaks module autonomy — both modules must coordinate schema changes | Each module owns its own tables; sync via events or API calls |\n| Putting business logic in a `@RestController` | Untestable and leaks domain logic into the HTTP layer | Controller delegates immediately to service; service owns all logic |\n| Creating an `[Entity]Service` that just wraps CRUD | Anemic domain model — no real encapsulation | Put behavior on the entity itself; service orchestrates multi-step use cases |\n| Publishing a Spring Event that contains a JPA `@Entity` | Entity may be a lazy-loaded proxy; causes serialization failures | Events carry only primitive values, IDs, or immutable value objects |\n| Adding a new enum to `shared/common/enums/` for a single-module concept | Pollutes shared space with module-specific concerns | Single-module enums stay inside that module's `domain/` package |\n| Writing business logic in `admin/` | Admin owns no domain; it becomes a second copy of truth | Admin delegates writes to the owning module's REST API; only reads and aggregates |\n| Admin calling a module's `@Service` for writes | Bypasses the module's own validation, events, and business rules | Admin must call the owning module's REST API for all write operations |\n| Mapping fields inside a `@Service` or `@RestController` | Pollutes orchestration/HTTP layers with translation logic; hard to test | All domain ↔ DTO mapping goes in `mapper/[Resource]Mapper` only |\n| Using `@CrossOrigin` on individual controllers | Scattered CORS config — inconsistent across modules | Define CORS once in `shared/config/WebConfig` |\n\n---\n\n## Event Naming Conventions\n\n```\nPublished events  →  Past tense + \"Event\" suffix\n                     [WhatHappened]Event\n                     e.g. BookingConfirmedEvent\n                          SeatReleasedEvent\n                          UserRegisteredEvent\n                          PaymentFailedEvent\n\nListeners         →  [TriggerEvent]Listener\n                     e.g. PaymentFailedListener (inside booking-inventory module)\n                          BookingConfirmedListener (inside engagement module)\n                          CartAssembledListener (inside payments module)\n\nEvent payload     →  Only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects.\n                     NEVER include @Entity objects in events.\n                     NEVER include mutable collections.\n```\n\n---\n\n## Module pom.xml Dependency Rules\n\n```xml\n<!-- Every module pom.xml follows this exact pattern -->\n<dependencies>\n\n  <!-- 1. Always: shared internal library -->\n  <dependency>\n    <groupId>com.[org]</groupId>\n    <artifactId>shared</artifactId>\n  </dependency>\n\n  <!-- 2. Only upstream modules (modules this one reads from) -->\n  <!-- Example: booking-inventory reads from discovery-catalog -->\n  <dependency>\n    <groupId>com.[org]</groupId>\n    <artifactId>[upstream-module]</artifactId>\n  </dependency>\n\n  <!-- 3. NEVER depend on a peer or downstream module -->\n  <!-- NEVER depend on admin/ -->\n  <!-- NEVER depend on app/ -->\n\n</dependencies>\n```\n\n---\n\n## Flyway Migration Naming\n\n```\nV[auto-increment]__[verb]_[table_name].sql\n\nExamples:\n"
  }
}
```

Delegates to service.\n    │   └── dto/\n    │       ├── request/           ← [Action][Resource]Request\n    │       └── response/          ← [Resource]Response. Flat, serializable, no JPA proxies.\n    │\n    ├── domain/                    ← Pure business logic. No Spring, no JPA, no infrastructure.\n    │                                Entities have behavior (confirm(), cancel()) not just getters.\n    │\n    ├── service/                   ← Orchestrates domain + repositories + ACL + events. @Service beans.\n    │                                One service per capability group. Methods = use cases not CRUD.\n    │\n    ├── repository/                ← @Repository interfaces only. No other module imports these.\n    │\n    ├── mapper/                    ← Domain ↔ DTO translation only. Use MapStruct.\n    │\n    ├── event/\n    │   ├── published/             ← Events this module fires. Past tense. Primitives/IDs only.\n    │   └── listener/              ← Events this module reacts to. No logic — delegate to service.\n    │\n    ├── statemachine/              ← OPTIONAL. Only if 4+ states with conditional transition guards.\n    │   ├── [Resource]StateMachineConfig\n    │   ├── [Resource]State\n    │   ├── [Resource]Event\n    │   ├── [Resource]StateMachineService\n    │   ├── guard/\n    │   └── action/\n    │\n    └── exception/                 ← OPTIONAL. Module-specific exceptions only.\n```\n\n---\n\n## admin/ and app/\n\n```\nadmin/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/admin/\n    ├── api/controller/            ← Admin-only endpoints (@PreAuthorize ROLE_ADMIN)\n    └── service/                   ← Aggregates reads across modules. Delegates writes to module APIs.\n\napp/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/\n    ├── [ProjectName]Application   ← @SpringBootApplication. One class, nothing else.\n    └── resources/\n        ├── application.yml\n        ├── application-local.yml\n        ├── application-prod.yml\n        └── db/migration/          ← Flyway migrations. Global version sequence. Never modify existing files.\n```\n\n---\n\n## Anti-Patterns — Never Do These\n\n| Anti-Pattern | Why It's Wrong | Correct Approach |\n|---|---|---|\n| Module A imports `@Service` from Module B | Direct coupling — changes in B break A at compile time | Module A calls Module B's REST endpoint or listens to its Spring Event |\n| Module A imports `@Entity` or `@Repository` from Module B | Hidden DB coupling — schema changes in B break A silently | Module A calls Module B's API to get the data it needs |\n| Putting Eventbrite/Razorpay HTTP calls inside a module's service | Scattered integration — if the API changes, you hunt across modules | All external calls go through `shared/[system-acl]/service/` facades only |\n| Sharing a database table between two modules | Breaks module autonomy — both modules must coordinate schema changes | Each module owns its own tables; sync via events or API calls |\n| Putting business logic in a `@RestController` | Untestable and leaks domain logic into the HTTP layer | Controller delegates immediately to service; service owns all logic |\n| Creating an `[Entity]Service` that just wraps CRUD | Anemic domain model — no real encapsulation | Put behavior on the entity itself; service orchestrates multi-step use cases |\n| Publishing a Spring Event that contains a JPA `@Entity` | Entity may be a lazy-loaded proxy; causes serialization failures | Events carry only primitive values, IDs, or immutable value objects |\n| Adding a new enum to `shared/common/enums/` for a single-module concept | Pollutes shared space with module-specific concerns | Single-module enums stay inside that module's `domain/` package |\n| Writing business logic in `admin/` | Admin owns no domain; it becomes a second copy of truth | Admin delegates writes to the owning module's REST API; only reads and aggregates |\n| Admin calling a module's `@Service` for writes | Bypasses the module's own validation, events, and business rules | Admin must call the owning module's REST API for all write operations |\n| Mapping fields inside a `@Service` or `@RestController` | Pollutes orchestration/HTTP layers with translation logic; hard to test | All domain ↔ DTO mapping goes in `mapper/[Resource]Mapper` only |\n| Using `@CrossOrigin` on individual controllers | Scattered CORS config — inconsistent across modules | Define CORS once in `shared/config/WebConfig` |\n\n---\n\n## Event Naming Conventions\n\n```\nPublished events  →  Past tense + \"Event\" suffix\n                     [WhatHappened]Event\n                     e.g. BookingConfirmedEvent\n                          SeatReleasedEvent\n                          UserRegisteredEvent\n                          PaymentFailedEvent\n\nListeners         →  [TriggerEvent]Listener\n                     e.g. PaymentFailedListener (inside booking-inventory module)\n                          BookingConfirmedListener (inside engagement module)\n                          CartAssembledListener (inside payments module)\n\nEvent payload     →  Only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects.\n                     NEVER include @Entity objects in events.\n                     NEVER include mutable collections.\n```\n\n---\n\n## Module pom.xml Dependency Rules\n\n```xml\n<!-- Every module pom.xml follows this exact pattern -->\n<dependencies>\n\n  <!-- 1. Always: shared internal library -->\n  <dependency>\n    <groupId>com.[org]</groupId>\n    <artifactId>shared</artifactId>\n  </dependency>\n\n  <!-- 2. Only upstream modules (modules this one reads from) -->\n  <!-- Example: booking-inventory reads from discovery-catalog -->\n  <dependency>\n    <groupId>com.[org]</groupId>\n    <artifactId>[upstream-module]</artifactId>\n  </dependency>\n\n  <!-- 3. NEVER depend on a peer or downstream module -->\n  <!-- NEVER depend on admin/ -->\n  <!-- NEVER depend on app/ -->\n\n</dependencies>\n```\n\n---\n\n## Flyway Migration Naming\n\n```\nV[auto-increment]__[verb]_[table_name].sql\n\nExamples:\n"
  }
}
```
---
## Entry 41
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T14:16:05.445Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T14:16:05.445Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_SNalFWb8w7aAqcLb5avhw6AC",
    "output": "Chunk ID: eeeba1\nWall time: 0.0506 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 2356\nOutput:\n# CODING_AGENT.md — Spring & Java Coding Standards\n\n> Read this file before writing any production code.\n> These are the coding standards for this codebase. Every class, method, and annotation\n> must follow these rules. When in doubt, refer to `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` for\n> where code lives and this file for how to write it.\n\n---\n\n## Before Writing Any Code — Checklist\n\n- [ ] Read `PRODUCT.md` — confirm which module owns this feature, check the inter-module event map and ACL facade names\n- [ ] Read the confirmed `SPEC.md` for this feature — domain model, API, business logic, error handling\n- [ ] Read `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` — confirm which package and layer each class belongs to\n- [ ] Read `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` — testing implementation/execution/logging ownership is delegated to the Test Agent\n\n### Scope Boundary for CODING_AGENT\n\n- CODING_AGENT owns **production code quality and architecture compliance**.\n- CODING_AGENT does **not** own end-to-end test authoring/execution workflow by default.\n- Test creation, execution, and failure/fix logging are owned by `agents/TEST_AGENT.md`.\n- If explicitly asked to write tests in the same task, follow `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` and coordinate with TEST_AGENT standards.\n\n---\n\n## Java & Spring Version Baseline\n\n```\nJava:          21 (use records, sealed classes, pattern matching, text blocks where appropriate)\nSpring Boot:   3.5.11\nSpring:        6.x\nMapStruct:     1.5.x\nLombok:        use sparingly — prefer Java records for DTOs and value objects\nJPA:           Jakarta Persistence (jakarta.persistence.*) — NOT javax.persistence\nFlyway:        version managed in parent pom.xml only\n```\n\n---\n\n## General Coding Rules\n\n### Immutability First\n\nPrefer immutable objects. Use Java records for DTOs, events, and value objects.\nOnly use mutable classes when the lifecycle explicitly requires mutation (e.g. JPA entities).\n\n```java\n// ✅ Correct — record for response DTO\npublic record BookingResponse(UUID id, String status, UUID userId) {}\n\n// ✅ Correct — record for event payload\npublic record BookingConfirmedEvent(UUID bookingId, UUID userId, UUID showSlotId) {}\n\n// ❌ Wrong — mutable class for a DTO\npublic class BookingResponse {\n    private UUID id;\n    public void setId(UUID id) { this.id = id; }\n}\n```\n\n### No Nulls in Public APIs\n\nUse `Optional<T>` for values that may be absent. Never return `null` from a public method.\nNever accept `null` as a valid input — validate at the boundary with `@NotNull`.\n\n```java\n// ✅ Correct\npublic Optional<Booking> findById(UUID id) {\n    return bookingRepository.findById(id);\n}\n\n// ❌ Wrong — null return leaks nullability into callers\npublic Booking findById(UUID id) {\n    return bookingRepository.findById(id).orElse(null);\n}\n```\n\n### Use `Money` for All Monetary Values\n\nNever use `double`, `float`, or raw `BigDecimal` for monetary amounts.\nAlways use the `Money` value object from `shared/common/domain/Money`.\n\n```java\n// ✅ Correct\nprivate final Money price;\n\n// ❌ Wrong\nprivate BigDecimal price;\nprivate double price;\n```\n\n### Logging\n\nUse SLF4J with `@Slf4j` (Lombok). Never use `System.out.println`.\nLog at the correct level:\n\n| Level | When to Use |\n|---|---|\n| `TRACE` | Verbose debug — method entry/exit during development only |\n| `DEBUG` | Internal state during a complex operation |\n| `INFO` | Significant business events: booking confirmed, payment received |\n| `WARN` | Recoverable issues: seat lock expired, retry attempted |\n| `ERROR` | Unrecoverable failures: payment charged but DB write failed |\n\nAlways include structured context. Use MDC for `traceId` and `requestId` (configured in `shared/config/TracingConfig`).\n\n```java\n// ✅ Correct\nlog.info(\"Booking confirmed. bookingId={} userId={} amount={}\", bookingId, userId, amount);\nlog.error(\"DB write failed after payment charge. bookingId={} paymentIntentId={}\", bookingId, paymentIntentId, e);\n\n// ❌ Wrong\nSystem.out.println(\"booking done\");\nlog.info(\"Booking confirmed: \" + bookingId); // string concatenation, not parameterised\n```\n\n---\n\n## Inter-Module Communication Patterns\n\nModules communicate via REST (sync queries) or Spring Events (async commands). Never import another module's @Service directly (exception: `admin/` may import module `@Service` for READ-only operations).\n\n**Pattern 1: REST Query** — Module B needs data from upstream Module A\n```java\n// ✅ Call REST API\nprivate final RestClient restClient;\npublic Payment getPaymentDetails(UUID paymentId) {\n    return restClient.get(\"/api/payments/{id}\", paymentId, Payment.class);\n}\n\n// ❌ Never import @Service\nprivate final PaymentService paymentService; // WRONG\n```\n\n**Pattern 2: Spring Events** — Module A publishes, others listen\n```java\n// Module A publishes\neventPublisher.publishEvent(new PaymentConfirmedEvent(bookingId, amount));\n\n// Module B listens (delegates immediately to service)\n@Component\npublic class PaymentConfirmedListener {\n    @TransactionalEventListener(phase = TransactionPhase.AFTER_COMMIT)\n    public void onPaymentConfirmed(PaymentConfirmedEvent event) {\n        bookingService.confirmBooking(event.bookingId());\n    }\n}\n\n// ❌ Never call service directly from event trigger\n// ❌ Never block the listener — delegate immediately\n```\n\n**Pattern 3: Shared Contracts** — Enums, DTOs, base classes in `shared/common/` only\n```java\n// ✅ Define once in shared/common/enums/\npublic enum BookingStatus { PENDING, CONFIRMED, CANCELLED, REFUNDED }\n\n// ❌ Never duplicate in multiple modules\n```\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facades — The Only Door to External Systems\n\n**HARD RULE: No module calls Eventbrite HTTP directly. All calls go through shared/eventbrite/service/ facades only.**\n\n### The 10 Eventbrite Facades (All in `shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n\n| Facade | Used By | Purpose |\n|---|---|---|\n| EbEventSyncService | discovery-catalog, scheduling, engagement | Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull catalog |\n| EbVenueService | scheduling | Create/update venues; list org venues |\n| EbScheduleService | scheduling | Create recurring event schedules |\n| EbTicketService | discovery-catalog, booking-inventory | Create/update ticket classes; check availability |\n| EbCapacityService | admin/ | Update event capacity tiers |\n| EbOrderService | payments-ticketing | Read orders post-checkout |\n| EbAttendeeService | payments-ticketing, engagement | Get attendees; verify attendance for reviews |\n| EbDiscountSyncService | promotions | Create/update/delete discount codes |\n| EbRefundService | payments-ticketing | Read refund status (no submission API) |\n| EbWebhookService | mock-eventbrite-api only | Mock/testing webhook registration and delivery simulation (NOT production) |\n\n### Correct Pattern\n\n```java\n// ✅ Inject facade, call it\n@Service\npublic class EventService {\n    private final EbEventSyncService ebEventService; // from shared/\n    \n    public Event createEvent(EventRequest request) {\n        Event event = new Event(request.name(), request.venue());\n        eventRepository.save(event); // save internally first\n        \n        EbEventResponse ebResp = ebEventService.createEvent(\n            mapper.toEbCreateRequest(event));\n        event.setEventbriteEventId(ebResp.id()); // store EB ID\n        eventRepository.save(event);\n        return event;\n    }\n}\n\n// ❌ Never import and call HTTP client directly\nprivate final EventbriteWebClient ebClient;\npublic void createEvent(...) {\n    ebClient.post(\"/events\", ...); // VIOLATES HARD RULE #2\n}\n```\n\n### Critical Constraints (These Don't Exist on Eventbrite)\n\n- **No user creation API** — Users 100% internal; link post-purchase via order email\n- **No order creation API** — JS SDK widget only; backend reads AFTER onOrderComplete callback\n- **No single-order cancel** — Only bulk event cancel; per-order requires workaround\n- **No seat lock API** — Entire state machine (AVAILABLE → SOFT_LOCKED → HARD_LOCKED → CONFIRMED) is internal Redis + Spring State Machine\n- **No refund submission API** — Refund status read-only via EbRefundService only\n- **No conflict validation API** — Turnaround gaps enforced entirely in internal DB\n- **No reviews API** — Reviews 100% internal; Eventbrite attendance verification only\n\n**See docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md for full constraints and workarounds.**\n\n---\n\n## admin/ Module Special Rules\n\nAdmin owns no domain, no @Entity classes, no database tables. It reads and orchestrates.\n\n### Reading from Modules — Direct Import OK\n\n```java\n// ✅ Reads only — can import @Service directly (same JVM)\n@Service\npublic class AdminDashboardService {\n    private final BookingService bookingService;  // from booking-inventory\n    private final PaymentService paymentService;  // from payments-ticketing\n    \n    public Dashboard getMetrics() {\n        return new Dashboard(\n            bookingService.getTotalBookings(),\n            paymentService.getTotalRevenue()\n        );\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Writing to Modules — REST API Only\n\n```java\n// ❌ WRONG — bypasses module's validation and events\nprivate final BookingRepository bookingRepository;\npublic void bulkCancel(...) {\n    bookingRepository.deleteAll(bookings); // skips business rules\n}\n\n// ✅ CORRECT — calls module's REST API\npublic void bulkCancel(List<UUID> bookingIds) {\n    for (UUID id : bookingIds) {\n        restClient.delete(\"/api/bookings/{id}\", id);\n    }\n}\n```\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 48
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T14:16:09.459Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T14:16:09.459Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_MUvvuq8c839ek9gHE2bxXmzg",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 51140e\nWall time: 0.0512 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 3261\nOutput:\n# PRODUCT.md — Module Registry & Product Map\n\n**Owner:** Lalit  \n**Domain:** EntertainmentTech  \n**Last Updated:** March 2026\n\n**Architecture:** Modular Monolith — Spring Boot 3.5.11, Java 21\n\n**External Integrations:** Eventbrite (ticketing + payments + orders) | OpenAI GPT-4o (AI assistant)\n\n⚠ **Important:** This is the single source of truth for module ownership, boundaries, inter-module communication, and the Eventbrite ACL. Every agent and developer MUST read this before touching any module.\n\n---\n\n## 1. The 7 Modules — What Each One Owns\n\n### Module Ownership\n\n| Module | Owns | Must Not Own |\n|---|---|---|\n| discovery-catalog | Events, Venues, Cities, Eventbrite catalog sync | Bookings, Users, Payments |\n| scheduling | Show slots, time slot config, conflict validation, Eventbrite event sync | Bookings, Users |\n| identity | Users, JWT auth, profiles, preferences | Bookings, Payments, Events |\n| booking-inventory | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Seat Lock State Machine, Pricing | Payments, Reviews, Users |\n| payments-ticketing | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | Seats (reads cart only) |\n| promotions | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules, EB discount sync | Bookings, Payments, Users |\n| engagement | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot, RAG pipeline | Bookings (reads attended status only) |\n\n- `admin/` — owns no domain. Orchestrates and aggregates across modules. No `@Entity` classes.\n- `shared/` — owns all external integrations (Eventbrite ACL), JWT, base classes, common DTOs.\n- `app/` — entry point only. `@SpringBootApplication`. Zero business logic.\n\n## 2. Module Dependency Order\n\n```text\nshared/               ← foundational — no dependency on any module\n    ↓\ndiscovery-catalog     ← depends on shared only\n    ↓\nscheduling            ← depends on shared + discovery-catalog (reads venue/event via REST)\n    ↓\nidentity              ← depends on shared only\n    ↓\nbooking-inventory     ← depends on shared + discovery-catalog (reads event/venue via REST)\n    ↓\npayments-ticketing    ← depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (reads via REST)\n    ↓\npromotions            ← depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (reads via REST)\n    ↓\nengagement            ← depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (reads via REST)\n    ↓\nadmin/                ← thin orchestration — reads from all modules, writes via REST APIs\n    ↓\napp/                  ← entry point only\n```\n\n**Rule:** A module NEVER imports from a module at the same level or below it. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports.\n\n## 3. How Modules Communicate — 3 Rules\n\n### Rule 1 — Never import another module's `@Service` or `@Repository` directly\nIf Module B needs data from Module A, it calls Module A's REST endpoint.\n\n### Rule 2 — Use Spring Events for async reactions\nWhen something important happens in Module A, it publishes a Spring `ApplicationEvent`. Other modules react by registering `@EventListener` methods. No direct calls.\n\n- booking-inventory → publishes `CartAssembledEvent`\n- payments-ticketing → listens `CartAssembledListener` → initiates payment\n\n- payments-ticketing → publishes `PaymentFailedEvent`\n- booking-inventory → listens `PaymentFailedListener` → triggers seat lock rollback\n\n- payments-ticketing → publishes `BookingConfirmedEvent`\n- engagement → listens `BookingConfirmedListener` → unlocks review eligibility\n- identity → listens `BookingConfirmedListener` → updates order history\n\n### Rule 3 — All Eventbrite API calls go through `shared/eventbrite/service/` only\nNo module ever calls Eventbrite directly. The ACL facade services are the only entry point.\nInbound Eventbrite webhooks: `EbWebhookController → EbWebhookDispatcher → dispatched as Spring Events → modules listen`.\nModules never consume the raw webhook directly.\n\n## 4. Eventbrite ACL — Facades in `shared/`\n\n### ACL Facade Services\n\n- **EbEventSyncService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create (org-scoped), update, publish, unpublish, cancel, delete events; pull org event catalog; verify sync integrity\n  \n- **EbVenueService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create venues under an org (`POST /organizations/{org_id}/venues/`); update venues (`POST /venues/{venue_id}/`); list org venues (`GET /organizations/{org_id}/venues/`); list events at a venue (`GET /venues/{venue_id}/events/`).\n  \n- **EbScheduleService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create recurring event schedules and occurrences; retrieve series info\n  \n- **EbTicketService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n  \n- **EbCapacityService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Retrieve and update event capacity tiers; list org seat maps; attach seat map to event\n  \n- **EbOrderService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Retrieve order details post-checkout; list orders by event/org. **NO order creation via API** — orders created exclusively by Checkout Widget.\n  \n- **EbAttendeeService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Retrieve attendee records; list attendees by event. **NO attendee creation via API** — attendees created by Checkout Widget.\n  \n- **EbDiscountSyncService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create/update/delete discount codes at org level; sync usage with internal promotions. (Discounts scoped to org, applied to events at checkout.)\n  \n- **EbRefundService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Read refund request status only from order `refund_request` field. **NO refund submission API.**\n  \n- **EbWebhookService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - **NOT USED in production.** Webhook registration not available via API; only via Dashboard. Mock service simulates webhook delivery for testing.\n\n> **Note:** `EbOrderService` does NOT create orders. Orders are created exclusively by the Eventbrite Checkout Widget (JS SDK). `EbOrderService` only reads orders after the widget fires the `onOrderComplete` callback.\n\n> **Note:** `EbAttendeeService` does NOT create attendees. Attendees are created automatically when the Checkout Widget completes an order. `EbAttendeeService` only reads attendee records.\n\n> **Note:** `EbRefundService` does NOT submit refunds programmatically. Refund status is read from the order's `refund_request` field only. Actual refund initiation is handled by mimicking admin actions via Eventbrite org token (backend only).\n\n> **Note:** `EbVenueService` supports venue creation (`POST /organizations/{org_id}/venues/`) and updates (`POST /venues/{venue_id}/`). The Eventbrite API v3 exposes both endpoints. Venue creation in your app always pushes to Eventbrite and stores the returned `eb_venue_id`.\n\n### Mock Eventbrite Service — Development & Testing\n\n**Location:** `mock-eventbrite-api/` (Python FastAPI, in-memory)\n\n**Purpose:** Enable local development and integration testing without making real API calls to production Eventbrite. All 10 ACL façades route through this mock service when `spring.profiles.active=mock`.\n\n**Architecture:** Python FastAPI server (async, non-blocking) provides full mock implementations of Eventbrite API v3 endpoints. Data is stored in-memory (no database persistence by default). For integration tests, add SQLite or PostgreSQL backend to the mock service.\n\n**Endpoints Mocked:**\n- `POST/GET /organizations/{org_id}/events/` \n- `POST/GET /venues/{venue_id}/events/`\n- `GET /events/{event_id}/`\n- `POST/GET /ticket_classes/`\n- `POST/GET /inventory_tiers/`\n- `POST/GET /capacity_tiers/`\n- `POST/GET /orders/`\n- `POST/GET /attendees/`\n- `POST/GET /discounts/`\n- `POST/GET /webhooks/` (webhook registration)\n- ... and 100+ supporting endpoints\n\n**Configuration:**\n- **Spring Profile:** `mock` (Spring Boot automatically routes all ACL façade calls to mock service)\n- **Environment Variable:** `EVENTBRITE_API_URL=http://localhost:9000/v3/` (when using mock profile)\n- **Startup Command:** \n  ```bash\n  cd mock-eventbrite-api\n  python -m uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 9000 --reload\n  ```\n\n**Trade-Offs:**\n| Aspect | Behavior |\n|---|---|\n| **Data Persistence** | In-memory; lost on shutdown. Acceptable for rapid iteration. |\n| **Latency** | Minimal (<5ms); no real network calls. |\n| **API Fidelity** | Matches Eventbrite API v3 schema exactly; test against real contract. |\n| **Concurrency** | Handles parallel requests; respects single-flight locks for testing. |\n| **Webhooks** | Simulates webhook delivery for testing event-driven flows. |\n\n**Usage Pattern:**\n- **Local Dev:** Run mock service; set `spring.profiles.active=mock` in `application-mock.properties`\n- **Integration Tests:** Use Spring `@ActiveProfiles(\"mock\")` to inject mock implementations\n- **Production:** Set `spring.profiles.active=production`; real Eventbrite API calls occur\n\n**See** `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` **for detailed mock service documentation, sample payloads, and configuration options.**\n\n## 5. Eventbrite Integration Principles\n\n**Core Architecture:** Your internal DB is the primary source of truth. Eventbrite is the ticketing/payment backbone. Every entity is created internally first — then pushed to Eventbrite. The `eventbrite_event_id` returned from each push is stored in your DB and is the bridge between both systems.\n\n### 5.1 Internal-First, Then Eventbrite\n\nThe pattern for every flow that touches Eventbrite is always:\n\n1. User/Admin performs action in YOUR app\n2. Internal DB is updated / validated first\n3. Eventbrite API is called via `shared/` ACL facade\n4. Eventbrite returns ID or confirmation\n5. Your DB stores the Eventbrite ID (`eventbrite_event_id`, `eventbrite_order_id`, etc.)\n6. Both systems are now in sync — your DB is source of truth\n\n### 5.2 The `eventbrite_event_id` is the Spine of the System\n\nEvery downstream flow depends on `eventbrite_event_id` being present and valid in your DB:\n- FR5 (Payment) — Eventbrite Checkout Widget needs a valid `event_id` to render\n- FR6 (Cancellation) — Eventbrite cancel API needs a valid event tied to a valid order\n- FR7 (Discounts) — Eventbrite discount codes are scoped to `event_id`\n- FR8 (Reviews) — Verifying attended booking requires a confirmed Eventbrite order under that `event_id`\n- FR10 (Seat Lock) — Availability check hits Eventbrite `ticket_classes` for that `event_id`\n\nWithout FR2 sync: If `eventbrite_event_id` is null or orphaned, the entire chain breaks. No tickets can be sold, no orders created, no refunds processed.\n\n### 5.3 What Eventbrite Cannot Do — Internal Responsibilities\n\n| Gaps | Handled Internally |\n|---|---|\n| User creation/sync | No user write API on Eventbrite. Identity is 100% internal. Users are linked via order email post-purchase. |\n| Conflict validation | No conflict API on Eventbrite. Time slot conflicts and turnaround gaps are enforced in your internal DB. |\n| \"Venue creation — POST /organizations/{org_id}/venues/ is available via EbVenueService.createVenue(). Admin creates venues in your app; they are pushed to Eventbrite and eb_venue_id is stored. Venue updates via POST /venues/{venue_id}/. The Eventbrite Dashboard can also manage venues independently — VenueReconciliationJob detects and flags drift.\"|\n| Seat locking | No seat lock API on Eventbrite. The entire state machine (Redis + Spring State Machine) is internal. |\n| Cart management | No cart API on Eventbrite. Cart assembly and group discount rules are internal. |\n| Order creation | No order creation API. Orders are created exclusively via the Eventbrite Checkout Widget (JS SDK). Backend only reads orders post-creation. |\n| Attendee creation | No attendee creation API. Attendees are created automatically by Checkout Widget when orders are placed. Backend only reads attendee records. |\n| Payment processing | Eventbrite Checkout Widget handles all payments (credit card, debit, UPI, Eventbrite wallet, PayPal, etc.). Backend reads orders post-creation via EbOrderService. |\n| Single order cancel | No per-order cancel API. Only full event cancel exists. Per-order cancellation is mimicked via org admin token. |\n| Refund submission | No programmatic refund API. Refund status is embedded in order `refund_request` field only. Read-only access. |\n| Webhook registration | No webhook API. Webhooks are registered via Eventbrite Dashboard only. This app simulates webhooks for testing/development. |\n| Reviews | No reviews API on Eventbrite. Entire review system is internal. Attendance verification uses attendee read API. |\n| Public event search | `GET /events/search/` is deprecated. Event discovery is limited to your org's events. |\n\n## 6. Functional Requirement Flows — Internal + Eventbrite Sync\n\n### FR1 — City Selection → Venue Discovery → Event Listing\n**Module:** discovery-catalog\n\nThis flow provides the entry point for users browsing events. The internal catalog is the primary source. Eventbrite contributes only events your org has created — public city-wide search is not available (deprecated API).\n\n"
  }
}
```
